title: Benko Gambit: Accepted
summary: Explore the Benko Gambit, where Black sacrifices a pawn for queenside pressure and piece activity.
description: The Benko Gambit is one of Black's most aggressive weapons. By sacrificing a pawn on b4, Black gains queenside pressure and piece activity. This tutorial covers the main line and typical patterns.
objectives: Understand the Benko Gambit: why the pawn sacrifice is worthwhile
Learn why Black gets queenside pressure and piece activity
plans: White's plan: Hold the extra pawn and use central control
Black's plan: Create pressure on the b-file and queenside
The move ...a6 and ...Bxa6 is the main line
White should avoid exchanging - keep the pressure
commonMistakes: Do not think the Benko is bad for Black - it's very dangerous
Avoid playing too passively - keep the initiative
Do not underestimate Black's queenside play - it's powerful
White should maintain central control and piece activity
completionSummary: The Benko Gambit is a sharp line. You've learned the main ideas and can play both sides.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center.
steps.coaching: The Benko Gambit is one of Black's most aggressive weapons.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.coaching: Black attacks d4 and prepares the Benko Gambit.

steps.title: Challenge the center
steps.coaching: This is the standard setup against the Benko.

steps.title: The Benko Gambit begins
steps.coaching: This is the Benko Gambit! Black attacks the center.

steps.title: White advances in the center
steps.instruction: Play d5 to create a large center.

steps.title: The gambit move
steps.instruction: Black pushes b5, sacrificing a pawn.
steps.coaching: This is the Benko Gambit! Black sacrifices for queenside pressure.

steps.title: White accepts the gambit
steps.instruction: Capture on b5 with the c-pawn.
steps.coaching: White accepts the pawn. Now Black gets initiative.
steps.hint: Capture on b5 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black pushes a6
steps.instruction: Black plays a6, preparing to regain the pawn.
steps.coaching: Black prepares ...Bxa6 to regain the pawn with activity.

steps.title: White pushes forward
steps.instruction: Capture on a6 with the b-pawn.
steps.coaching: White pushes the pawn, creating a protected passed pawn.
steps.hint: Capture on a6 with the b-pawn.

steps.title: Black regains the pawn
steps.instruction: Capture on a6 with the bishop.
steps.coaching: Black regains the pawn with a powerful bishop on a6.
steps.hint: Capture on a6 with the bishop.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: White develops the knight, controlling d5.

steps.title: Black supports the center
steps.coaching: Black strengthens the center and prepares development.

steps.title: Prepare the bishop
steps.coaching: White prepares the fianchetto, controlling the long diagonal.

steps.title: Black plays g6
steps.instruction: Black plays g6, setting up the fianchetto.
steps.coaching: Black is setting up a King's Indian-style structure.

steps.title: Develop the bishop
steps.coaching: White develops the bishop, controlling the long diagonal.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.coaching: Black develops the bishop, completing the fianchetto.
